steps.title: Identify the defining contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts realism—which accurately represents and shows imperfection—with romanticism, which exaggerates beauty and hides imperfection. The realist style addresses what are explicitly called the “excesses” of romanticism. The blank describes what the realist style does to these excesses.

steps.title: Match the oppositional action
steps.content: ”Counteract” means to act against or oppose. By accurately representing subjects without exaggeration or concealment, the realist style directly counteracts the romantic style's excesses. Understand suggests passive observation, advance would mean promoting the problem, and accentuate would mean emphasizing it—none capture the oppositional stance the passage requires. That matches choice C — C is correct.
